checks.a_analysis: The output JSON faithfully reflects the body text and headings present in the source document. No fabricated paragraphs, sentences, or section headings were found. The placeholder dots ('.') in the source are correctly represented in the output.
No contact information, such as phone numbers, addresses, or URLs, has been fabricated in the output. The document metadata and body text do not contain any invented contact details.

All pages and main sections of the source document are represented in the output. The text from the first page (7.02c), second page (vi-viii and following paragraphs), and third page (section 7.02d) is included. Note that the repetitive footer information containing contact details is omitted from the JSON elements, which is generally considered an acceptable change for document metadata in this context.
The source document does not contain any tables; therefore, this check is not applicable.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: There are no significant omissions of text content within the sections. The word count in the metadata (868) aligns with the source document. While there are formatting issues in the red-line edited areas, the text itself is present.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The output contains several minor text defects resulting from a failure to correctly parse red-line edits (underlines and strikethroughs) on pages 1 and 2. In several instances, words were concatenated incorrectly, and text intended to be deleted (strikethrough) was included as plain active text. In item (i), 'under' (struck) and 'described' (underlined) were merged into 'underdescribed'. In item (ii), 'for' (struck) and 'of' (underlined) were merged into 'forof'. In item (i), 'Plan' (underlined) and 'previously' (struck) were merged into 'Planpreviously' (missing space). Furthermore, item (i) includes an entire clause that was struck through in the source as active plain text. These errors alter the intended meaning of the amended document.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 1: (i) ... deductible ~~under~~ _described_ ... the recipient is a primary Beneficiary _under the Plan_ ~~previously incurred ... medical care;~~. Page 1: (ii) ... residence ~~for~~ _of_ the Member.
checks.b_evidence_output: Item (i): '...deductible underdescribed...', '...Beneficiary under the Planpreviously incurred...', '...obtain such medical care;'. Item (ii): '...residence forof the Member'.

checks.a_analysis: The output document does not contain duplicated content. While the header and footer text ('3200 ATLANTIC AVENUE...') appears on every page in the source PDF and its OCR, the conversion pipeline has correctly omitted these repetitive elements from the semantic structure, including only the logo once at the start. Textual content of paragraphs and lists is unique and matches the count in the source.

The heading hierarchy is consistent. The document title is correctly identified as the section title. The main section heading '7.02 Hardship Withdrawal' is assigned level 2, which is an appropriate nesting under the document title. There are no skipped levels.
The source document contains no tables. The output correctly reflects this by not including any table elements.

The reading order in the JSON follows the logical top-to-bottom, page-by-page flow of the source document. It correctly handles the continuation of lists across page breaks (e.g., list item (v) continuing from page 1 to 2) and the insertion of paragraphs between list sections (c) and (d).

The list structure is generally well-preserved with correct nesting for sections (c) and (d). However, there is a minor defect in how the content of list items is handled when the source contains revision marks (strikethroughs and insertions). In list items (i) and (ii) on page 1, the struck-through text and the inserted text are merged into a single string without spaces, resulting in run-on words like 'Planpreviously' and 'forof'. This represents an incorrect merging of distinct textual versions within a list item.
checks.b_evidence_source: Item (i): '...recipient is a primary Beneficiary under the Plan' [inserted] 'previously incurred by the Member...' [struck through]. Item (ii): '...principal residence for' [struck through] 'of' [inserted] 'the Member'.
checks.b_evidence_output: Item (i): '...the recipient is a primary Beneficiary under the Planpreviously incurred by the Member...'. Item (ii): '...principal residence forof the Member...'.

checks.a_analysis: There are no links injected or missing in the document content. The URL in the header/footer (www.myncretirement.com) is correctly captured in the metadata but is not represented as a link within the semantic body, which is consistent with the exclusion of repetitive header/footer text.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML document faithfully replicates the visual organization of the source PDF. The hierarchical structure of the list items (i) through (viii) under section 7.02(c) and sub-item (ii) under 7.02(d) is maintained. While the HTML version utilizes a single-column responsive layout and includes additional list numbering (e.g., '1. (i)' instead of just '(i)'), the logical flow and relative placement of content blocks match the source. The header logos and footer text are also correctly positioned relative to the body content.
There are no tables present in the source PDF or the rendered HTML document.

The meaningful images from the source PDF, specifically the North Carolina Total Retirement Plans logo, the state seal, and the signature/seal for Dale R. Folwell, are all present and correctly rendered at the top of the HTML document.

A thorough visual scan of the HTML output confirms that no body content, paragraphs, or list items are duplicated. Each section from the PDF appears exactly once in the HTML version, following the correct sequence.
